# Appendix C. Stan Model Listings {#sec-appendix-c .unnumbered}

> *Full listings of the Stan programs referenced by the brief code excerpts in
> §[4](#sec-m0-implementation) and §[6](#sec-m1-implementation). Two model pairs
> are used throughout. For each model there is an **inference** program (`.stan`)
> that takes observed choices as data and samples the posterior, and a matched
> **simulation** program (`_sim.stan`) that draws parameters from the prior and
> generates synthetic choices in `generated quantities` --- the data-generating
> half of every prior-predictive, parameter-recovery, and SBC run. The
> calibrated-prior variant `m_01` used in the application (§[7](#sec-application))
> is structurally identical to `m_0` and is given as a one-line prior diff.
> Listings reflect the pinned commit of Appendix E.0; the canonical files live at
> `models/` in the repository.*

The notation matches §[2.6](#sec-notation): `alpha` is the sensitivity $\alpha$,
`beta` the belief-feature matrix $\beta$, `delta` the utility increments $\delta$
(a `simplex`, so `upsilon` $= \bm{\upsilon}$ is ordered with the endpoint
convention $\upsilon_1 = 0$ built in via `cumulative_sum(append_row(0, delta))`),
`psi` the per-alternative belief simplices $\bm{\psi}_r$, `eta` the expected
utilities $\eta_r$, and `chi` the per-problem choice probabilities. The `I`
(uncertain) and `J` (risky) indicator arrays encode which alternatives appear in
each problem; choices are `y` (uncertain) and `z` (risky).

## C.1 `m_0.stan` --- uncertain-choice-only inference model {#sec-c-m0 .unnumbered}

```stan
data{
  int<lower=1> M; // the number of decision problems
  int<lower=2> K; // the number of possible consequences
  int<lower=1> D; // the number of dimensions to describe an alternative
  int<lower=2> R; // the number of distinct alternatives
  array[R] vector[D] w; // the descriptions of each distinct alternative
  array[M,R] int<lower=0,upper=1> I; // I[m,r] = 1 if alternative r is in problem m
  array[M] int<lower=1> y; // selected alternative, as a LOCAL index into problem m's
                           // menu: y[m] in 1..N[m] gives the position (in ascending
                           // global-r order, matching the compacted x rows) of the
                           // chosen alternative, NOT a global index in 1..R
                           // (bounds checked in transformed data)
}
transformed data {
  array[M] int<lower=2> N; // the number of alternatives in each decision problem
  int total_alternatives = 0;
  for (m in 1:M) {
    N[m] = sum(I[m]);
    total_alternatives += N[m];
    if (y[m] > N[m]) reject("y[", m, "] = ", y[m], " must be <= N[", m, "] = ", N[m]);
  }
  // Construct x from w and I
  array[total_alternatives] vector[D] x;
  {
    int pos = 1;
    for (m in 1:M) {
      for (r in 1:R) {
        if (I[m, r] == 1) { x[pos] = w[r]; pos += 1; }
      }
    }
  }
}
parameters{
  real<lower=0> alpha; // sensitivity parameter (decision noise); strictly positive
  matrix[K,D] beta;    // coefficients mapping descriptions to subjective probabilities
  simplex[K - 1] delta;// utility differences on unit scale (ensures ordered utilities)
}
transformed parameters{
  array[sum(N)] simplex[K] psi; // subjective probability over outcomes per alternative
  ordered[K] upsilon;           // the subjective utility of each consequence
  vector[sum(N)] eta;           // expected utility of each alternative on unit scale
  array[M] simplex[max(N)] chi; // choice probs with padding
  for(i in 1:sum(N)){ psi[i] = softmax(beta*x[i]); }
  upsilon = cumulative_sum(append_row(0, delta));
  for(i in 1:sum(N)){ eta[i] = dot_product(psi[i],upsilon); }
  {
    int pos = 1;
    for(i in 1:M){
      vector[N[i]] problem_eta = segment(eta, pos, N[i]);
      chi[i] = append_row(softmax(alpha * problem_eta), rep_vector(0, max(N) - N[i]));
      pos += N[i];
    }
  }
}
model{
  alpha ~ lognormal(0, 1);              // prior on choice sensitivity
  to_vector(beta) ~ std_normal();        // prior on belief-feature coefficients
  delta ~ dirichlet(rep_vector(1,K-1));  // prior on ordered unit-scale utility increments
  for(i in 1:M){ y[i] ~ categorical(chi[i]); }
}
generated quantities {
  vector[M] log_lik;
  for (i in 1:M) { log_lik[i] = categorical_lpmf(y[i] | chi[i]); }
  array[M] int y_pred;
  for (i in 1:M) { y_pred[i] = categorical_rng(chi[i]); }

  // Posterior-predictive check statistics (one-sided Bayesian p-values):
  // log-likelihood discrepancy, modal-choice accuracy, and sum of chosen
  // probabilities, each comparing T(y_pred) to T(y_obs).
  real T_obs_ll = sum(log_lik);
  real T_rep_ll = 0;
  for (m in 1:M) { T_rep_ll += categorical_lpmf(y_pred[m] | chi[m]); }
  int<lower=0,upper=1> ppc_ll = (T_rep_ll >= T_obs_ll) ? 1 : 0;

  int T_obs_modal = 0; int T_rep_modal = 0;
  for (m in 1:M) {
    real max_prob = chi[m][1];
    for (j in 2:N[m]) { if (chi[m][j] > max_prob) max_prob = chi[m][j]; }
    T_obs_modal += (chi[m][y[m]]      >= max_prob - 1e-9) ? 1 : 0;
    T_rep_modal += (chi[m][y_pred[m]] >= max_prob - 1e-9) ? 1 : 0;
  }
  int<lower=0,upper=1> ppc_modal = (T_rep_modal >= T_obs_modal) ? 1 : 0;

  real T_obs_prob = 0; real T_rep_prob = 0;
  for (m in 1:M) { T_obs_prob += chi[m][y[m]]; T_rep_prob += chi[m][y_pred[m]]; }
  int<lower=0,upper=1> ppc_prob = (T_rep_prob >= T_obs_prob) ? 1 : 0;
}
```

## C.2 `m_0_sim.stan` --- uncertain-choice data generator {#sec-c-m0-sim .unnumbered}

The simulation program shares `m_0`'s `data`/`transformed data` shape but draws
$(\alpha, \beta, \delta)$ from the prior inside `generated quantities` (with the
$\alpha$-prior hyperparameters passed as data, so a single program serves both
the `Lognormal(0,1)` default and any calibrated prior), simulates choices `y`,
and reports `total_seu_max_selected` --- the count of problems in which the
sampled choice coincided with the SEU-maximizing alternative, the prior-predictive
diagnostic of §[4.2](#sec-m0-prior).

```stan
data {
  int<lower=1> M; int<lower=2> K; int<lower=1> D; int<lower=2> R;
  array[R] vector[D] w;
  array[M,R] int<lower=0,upper=1> I;
  real alpha_mean;          // mean for log(alpha) (default 0); may be negative
  real<lower=0> alpha_sd;   // sd for log(alpha)   (default 1)
  real<lower=0> beta_sd;    // sd for beta coefficients (default 1)
}
transformed data {
  array[M] int<lower=2> N; int total_alts = 0;
  for (m in 1:M) { N[m] = sum(I[m]); total_alts += N[m]; }
  array[total_alts] vector[D] x;
  {
    int pos = 1;
    for (m in 1:M) for (r in 1:R) if (I[m, r] == 1) { x[pos] = w[r]; pos += 1; }
  }
}
generated quantities {
  real alpha = lognormal_rng(alpha_mean, alpha_sd);
  matrix[K,D] beta;
  for (k in 1:K) for (d in 1:D) beta[k,d] = normal_rng(0, beta_sd);
  simplex[K-1] delta = dirichlet_rng(rep_vector(1.0, K-1));

  array[total_alts] vector[K] psi;
  for (i in 1:total_alts) psi[i] = softmax(beta * x[i]);
  vector[K] upsilon = cumulative_sum(append_row(0, delta));
  vector[total_alts] eta;
  for (i in 1:total_alts) eta[i] = dot_product(to_vector(psi[i]), upsilon);

  array[M] int y;
  array[M] int<lower=0,upper=1> selected_seu_max;
  {
    int pos = 1;
    for (i in 1:M) {
      vector[N[i]] problem_eta = segment(eta, pos, N[i]);
      vector[N[i]] choice_probs = softmax(alpha * problem_eta);
      y[i] = categorical_rng(choice_probs);
      real max_eta = max(problem_eta);
      selected_seu_max[i] = (abs(problem_eta[y[i]] - max_eta) < 1e-10) ? 1 : 0;
      pos += N[i];
    }
  }
  int<lower=0,upper=M> total_seu_max_selected = sum(selected_seu_max);
}
```

*(The full file additionally stores the per-problem `problem_etas` and
`choice_probabilities` for inspection; those bookkeeping arrays are elided here.)*

## C.3 `m_1.stan` --- combined uncertain + risky inference model {#sec-c-m1 .unnumbered}

`m_1` adds a risky block: a second set of `N` problems over `S` lotteries with
*objective* probability simplices `x` supplied as data. The lotteries enter
expected utility directly (`eta_risky = dot_product(x_risky, upsilon)`), with no
$\beta$ --- this $\beta$-free channel is what identifies $\delta$ in principle
(§[5](#sec-m1-identifiability), Proposition B.3). Crucially, `alpha` and the
utility vector `upsilon` are **shared** across the two blocks.

```stan
data {
  // Uncertain problems
  int<lower=1> M; int<lower=2> K; int<lower=1> D; int<lower=2> R;
  array[R] vector[D] w;
  array[M,R] int<lower=0,upper=1> I;
  array[M] int<lower=1> y;
  // Risky problems
  int<lower=1> N; int<lower=2> S;
  array[S] simplex[K] x;               // objective lottery simplices
  array[N,S] int<lower=0,upper=1> J;
  array[N] int<lower=1> z;
}
transformed data {
  array[M] int<lower=2> N_uncertain; int total_uncertain_alts = 0;
  for (m in 1:M) {
    N_uncertain[m] = sum(I[m]); total_uncertain_alts += N_uncertain[m];
    if (y[m] > N_uncertain[m]) reject("y[", m, "] out of range");
  }
  array[N] int<lower=2> N_risky; int total_risky_alts = 0;
  for (n in 1:N) {
    N_risky[n] = sum(J[n]); total_risky_alts += N_risky[n];
    if (z[n] > N_risky[n]) reject("z[", n, "] out of range");
  }
  array[total_uncertain_alts] vector[D] x_uncertain;
  {
    int pos = 1;
    for (m in 1:M) for (r in 1:R) if (I[m,r]==1) { x_uncertain[pos]=w[r]; pos+=1; }
  }
  array[total_risky_alts] simplex[K] x_risky;
  {
    int pos = 1;
    for (n in 1:N) for (s in 1:S) if (J[n,s]==1) { x_risky[pos]=x[s]; pos+=1; }
  }
}
parameters {
  real<lower=0> alpha;   // shared sensitivity
  matrix[K,D] beta;      // belief features (uncertain block only)
  simplex[K-1] delta;    // shared utility increments
}
transformed parameters {
  ordered[K] upsilon = cumulative_sum(append_row(0, delta)); // shared utilities

  // Uncertain block
  array[total_uncertain_alts] simplex[K] psi;
  vector[total_uncertain_alts] eta_uncertain;
  array[M] simplex[max(N_uncertain)] chi_uncertain;
  for (i in 1:total_uncertain_alts) psi[i] = softmax(beta * x_uncertain[i]);
  for (i in 1:total_uncertain_alts) eta_uncertain[i] = dot_product(psi[i], upsilon);
  {
    int pos = 1;
    for (m in 1:M) {
      vector[N_uncertain[m]] pe = segment(eta_uncertain, pos, N_uncertain[m]);
      chi_uncertain[m] = append_row(softmax(alpha*pe),
                                    rep_vector(0, max(N_uncertain)-N_uncertain[m]));
      pos += N_uncertain[m];
    }
  }

  // Risky block (beta-free: lotteries enter EU directly)
  vector[total_risky_alts] eta_risky;
  array[N] simplex[max(N_risky)] chi_risky;
  for (i in 1:total_risky_alts) eta_risky[i] = dot_product(x_risky[i], upsilon);
  {
    int pos = 1;
    for (n in 1:N) {
      vector[N_risky[n]] pe = segment(eta_risky, pos, N_risky[n]);
      chi_risky[n] = append_row(softmax(alpha*pe),
                                rep_vector(0, max(N_risky)-N_risky[n]));
      pos += N_risky[n];
    }
  }
}
model {
  alpha ~ lognormal(0, 1);
  to_vector(beta) ~ std_normal();
  delta ~ dirichlet(rep_vector(1, K-1));
  for (m in 1:M) y[m] ~ categorical(chi_uncertain[m]);
  for (n in 1:N) z[n] ~ categorical(chi_risky[n]);
}
generated quantities {
  vector[M] log_lik_uncertain;
  vector[N] log_lik_risky;
  for (m in 1:M) log_lik_uncertain[m] = categorical_lpmf(y[m] | chi_uncertain[m]);
  for (n in 1:N) log_lik_risky[n]     = categorical_lpmf(z[n] | chi_risky[n]);
  real log_lik_total = sum(log_lik_uncertain) + sum(log_lik_risky);
  array[M] int y_pred; array[N] int z_pred;
  for (m in 1:M) y_pred[m] = categorical_rng(chi_uncertain[m]);
  for (n in 1:N) z_pred[n] = categorical_rng(chi_risky[n]);
  // Elided for space: the PPC indicator statistics. m_1.stan computes, per
  // posterior draw, replicate-vs-observed indicators ppc_ll_*, ppc_modal_*,
  // ppc_prob_* (log-likelihood, modal-choice frequency, mean predicted
  // probability of the chosen alternative) built exactly as in m_0.stan (C.1),
  // once for the uncertain block, once for the risky block, and combined
  // (e.g. ppc_ll_combined from the summed block log-likelihoods) -- seven
  // indicators in all; see models/m_1.stan at the E.0 pin for the verbatim block.
}
```

## C.4 `m_1_sim.stan` --- combined data generator {#sec-c-m1-sim .unnumbered}

The matched simulator draws shared $(\alpha, \delta)$ and uncertain-only $\beta$
from the prior, then generates both `y` and `z`. The four matched-design
conditions of §[6.2](#sec-m1-matched) (Appendix D.4) are produced by slicing the
choices this program generates --- the same simulated parameters feed every
condition, which is what makes the A/B/C/D comparison a *matched* one.

```stan
data {
  int<lower=1> M; int<lower=2> K; int<lower=1> D; int<lower=2> R;
  array[R] vector[D] w; array[M,R] int<lower=0,upper=1> I;
  int<lower=1> N; int<lower=2> S;
  array[S] simplex[K] x; array[N,S] int<lower=0,upper=1> J;
  real alpha_mean; real<lower=0> alpha_sd; real<lower=0> beta_sd;
}
transformed data {
  array[M] int<lower=2> N_uncertain; int total_uncertain_alts = 0;
  for (m in 1:M) { N_uncertain[m] = sum(I[m]); total_uncertain_alts += N_uncertain[m]; }
  array[total_uncertain_alts] vector[D] x_uncertain;
  { int pos=1; for (m in 1:M) for (r in 1:R) if (I[m,r]==1){x_uncertain[pos]=w[r];pos+=1;} }
  array[N] int<lower=2> N_risky; int total_risky_alts = 0;
  for (n in 1:N) { N_risky[n] = sum(J[n]); total_risky_alts += N_risky[n]; }
  array[total_risky_alts] simplex[K] x_risky;
  { int pos=1; for (n in 1:N) for (s in 1:S) if (J[n,s]==1){x_risky[pos]=x[s];pos+=1;} }
}
generated quantities {
  real alpha = lognormal_rng(alpha_mean, alpha_sd);
  matrix[K,D] beta;
  for (k in 1:K) for (d in 1:D) beta[k,d] = normal_rng(0, beta_sd);
  simplex[K-1] delta = dirichlet_rng(rep_vector(1.0, K-1));
  vector[K] upsilon = cumulative_sum(append_row(0, delta));

  // Uncertain choices
  array[total_uncertain_alts] vector[K] psi;
  for (i in 1:total_uncertain_alts) psi[i] = softmax(beta * x_uncertain[i]);
  vector[total_uncertain_alts] eta_uncertain;
  for (i in 1:total_uncertain_alts) eta_uncertain[i] = dot_product(to_vector(psi[i]), upsilon);
  array[M] int y; array[M] int<lower=0,upper=1> selected_seu_max_uncertain;
  {
    int pos = 1;
    for (m in 1:M) {
      vector[N_uncertain[m]] pe = segment(eta_uncertain, pos, N_uncertain[m]);
      vector[N_uncertain[m]] cp = softmax(alpha * pe);
      y[m] = categorical_rng(cp);
      selected_seu_max_uncertain[m] = (abs(pe[y[m]] - max(pe)) < 1e-10) ? 1 : 0;
      pos += N_uncertain[m];
    }
  }

  // Risky choices (beta-free)
  vector[total_risky_alts] eta_risky;
  for (i in 1:total_risky_alts) eta_risky[i] = dot_product(to_vector(x_risky[i]), upsilon);
  array[N] int z; array[N] int<lower=0,upper=1> selected_seu_max_risky;
  {
    int pos = 1;
    for (n in 1:N) {
      vector[N_risky[n]] pe = segment(eta_risky, pos, N_risky[n]);
      vector[N_risky[n]] cp = softmax(alpha * pe);
      z[n] = categorical_rng(cp);
      selected_seu_max_risky[n] = (abs(pe[z[n]] - max(pe)) < 1e-10) ? 1 : 0;
      pos += N_risky[n];
    }
  }
  int total_seu_max_selected =
      sum(selected_seu_max_uncertain) + sum(selected_seu_max_risky);
}
```

## C.5 `m_01.stan` --- calibrated-prior variant (application) {#sec-c-m01 .unnumbered}

The application model `m_01` is **structurally identical** to `m_0` (§[C.1](#sec-c-m0))
--- same `data`, `parameters`, `transformed parameters`, and `generated
quantities`. It differs only in the $\alpha$ prior, which is calibrated by the
prior-predictive SEU-max-rate procedure of §[7.3](#sec-app-model) (claim C8)
rather than left at the convenience default. The single changed line in the
`model` block is:

```stan
// m_0.stan:
alpha ~ lognormal(0, 1);     // convenience default
// m_01.stan:
alpha ~ lognormal(3.0, 0.75); // calibrated: median ~ 20, 90% CI ~ [5.5, 67],
                              // prior-implied SEU-max rate ~ 0.78 (insurance, K=3)
```

For the $K = 4$ Ellsberg cells the same SEU-max-rate calibration is re-run to set
the $\alpha$-prior hyperparameters at that menu size (Appendix D.5); the model
code is otherwise unchanged.
